title: US forces enter Iran by..?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify, however intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US special forces conducted ground raids into Iran on April 5-6 to rescue downed airmen from US jets shot down during airstrikes, marking the first confirmed entries of American troops on Iranian soil since the February 28 launch of joint US-Israel air and missile campaigns targeting Iran's nuclear sites, leadership, and military infrastructure. The operation succeeded without reported casualties but underscores escalation risks in the ongoing 2026 Iran war, now in its sixth week, focused on air and naval dominance rather than full invasion. Iran rejected a US ceasefire proposal on April 6 to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, prompting Trump administration warnings of intensified strikes on energy infrastructure; Defense Secretary Hegseth announced the largest strike volume yet for April 8, while diplomatic channels remain open amid proxy attacks by Iranian allies.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain edges trader consensus as the slim 2026 FIFA World Cup favorite at 16% implied probability following their dramatic penalty shootout victory over Netherlands in the March European playoff second leg, capping a flawless qualifiers run that underscores their Euro 2024-winning momentum with stars like Yamal and Pedri in peak form. France trails closely at 13.6%, buoyed by topping FIFA rankings and recent Nations League dominance, while England's 11.3% reflects consistent qualification ease and Bellingham's emergence. Argentina (9.2%) and Brazil (8.6%) hold firm on Copa América pedigree and South American qualifier supremacy, keeping the race tight amid balanced group draws, deep European and South American talent pools, and no dominant injury crises—highlighting the expanded 48-team format's upset potential where recent friendlies like Spain's 3-0 Serbia win maintain parity among top contenders.

eventMetadata.context_description: California Governor Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination with a 24% implied probability, driven by his executive experience, robust fundraising, and frequent national critiques of President Trump since the party's 2024 defeat. Georgia Senator Jon Ossoff has climbed to third at 7% following his viral February anti-Trump Senate speech, praised for Obama-like appeal in the battleground state and potential bipartisan crossover. Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez maintains 8% on progressive enthusiasm and youth, while former Vice President Kamala Harris lags at 4% amid post-loss scrutiny. title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another, or for an official ceasefire agreement to be otherwise confirmed to have been reached by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Iran rejected a US ceasefire proposal via Pakistan earlier today, insisting on a permanent end to hostilities rather than a temporary truce that would favor American leverage, as President Trump escalates threats to strike Iran's power plants and infrastructure by tomorrow's deadline. This follows stalled diplomacy, including a 15-point plan and the proposed "Islamabad Accord" trading nuclear limits for sanctions relief, amid ongoing US-Israel airstrikes since late February that have targeted Tehran's leadership, nuclear sites, and missile capabilities. Strait of Hormuz tensions persist, with no immediate de-escalation signals; traders monitor for mediation breakthroughs or retaliatory actions that could tip escalation risks higher before resolution deadlines.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance leads narrowly as trader consensus favorite at 17.6% implied probability for the 2028 presidential winner, buoyed by his position as heir apparent in a Republican Party fresh off the 2024 victory and recent comments dismissing Democratic challengers like Gavin Newsom and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez as policy mismatches for working-class voters. California Governor Newsom trails closely at 16.8%, reflecting Democratic soul-searching and his high-profile national positioning amid party debates over direction post-Harris defeat. Marco Rubio's 10.3% share stems from his Secretary of State role and rising appeal in early betting shifts. This tight clustering underscores an open field two years pre-primaries, with 2026 midterms poised to elevate standout performers via swing-state wins or fundraising surges, while Trump endorsements or scandals could rapidly separate frontrunners.
